name: Golghar

name: Takht Sri Patna Sahib

name: Bihar Museum
description: Modern museum showcasing Bihar’s rich history and culture.

name: Mahabodhi Temple

name: Great Buddha Statue
description: 80-foot tall statue of Lord Buddha in meditation posture.

name: Vishnupad Temple

name: Nalanda University Ruins
description: Ancient center of learning dating back to 5th century CE.

name: Vishwa Shanti Stupa

name: Rajgir Hot Springs
description: Natural hot water springs believed to have medicinal properties.

name: Ashoka Pillar
description: Ancient pillar erected by Emperor Ashoka, symbol of Buddhism.

description: Magnificent sandstone tomb of Sher Shah Suri built in Indo-Islamic style.

name: Munger Fort
description: Historic fort on the banks of the Ganga River.

name: Janaki Temple

name: Hundru Falls

name: Dassam Falls
description: Beautiful waterfall on the Kanchi River, popular picnic spot.

name: Jonha Falls

name: Dimna Lake

name: Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Famous wildlife sanctuary known for elephants and scenic hills.

name: Maithon Dam
description: Large dam built on Barakar River, famous for boating and scenic beauty.

name: Panchet Dam
description: Multipurpose dam on Damodar River with beautiful sunset views.

name: Canary Hill
description: Hilltop viewpoint offering panoramic views of the town and forests.

name: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Wildlife reserve known for leopards, deer and scenic forest drives.

name: Suryakund
description: Natural hot water spring believed to have medicinal properties.

name: Hazaribagh lake
description: Beautiful lake surrounded by lush greenery, perfect for boating and picnics.

name: Baidyanath Temple

name: Trikut Hills
description: Scenic hill range known for trekking and ropeway ride.

name: Netarhat Sunrise Point

name: Palamu Fort

name: Golden Temple

name: Rose Garden

description: Museum showcasing rural Punjabi heritage and farming history.

name: Qila Mubarak
description: Historic fort complex representing Sikh architecture and royal heritage.

name: Sheesh Mahal

name: Takht Sri Kesgarh Sahib
description: One of the five Takhts of Sikhism and birthplace of the Khalsa.

description: One of the oldest surviving forts in India with historic significance.

name: Jagatjit Palace

name: Ropar Wetland

name: Vittala Temple

name: Panambur Beach

name: Kadri Manjunath Temple

name: Udupi Sri Krishna Temple
description: Famous pilgrimage center dedicated to Lord Krishna.

name: Jog Falls

name: Belagavi Fort

name: Unkal Lake

name: Ballari Fort
description: Historic hill fort offering panoramic views of the city.

description: One of India's oldest zoos set in lush forest surroundings, home to diverse wildlife species.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat

name: Hawa Mahal

name: City Palace

name: Lake Pichola
description: Beautiful artificial freshwater lake surrounded by palaces and temples, home to the Lake Palace hotel.

name: Mehrangarh Fort

name: Adalaj Stepwell

name: Laxmi Vilas Palace

name: Dumas Beach

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
description: UNESCO World Heritage baroque masterpiece from 1605 housing remains of St. Francis Xavier.

name: Fort Aguada
description: 17th-century Portuguese fort with a lighthouse offering spectacular coastal views.

name: Colva Beach
description: One of Goa's longest and most beautiful white-sand beaches stretching over 2.4 km.

name: Fort Kochi Beach
description: Charming beach with iconic Chinese fishing nets, colonial architecture, and vibrant art scene.

name: Mattancherry Palace
description: 16th-century Dutch Palace with stunning Kerala mural paintings depicting Hindu mythology.

name: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
description: Ancient Hindu temple known for Dravidian architecture and immense treasure vault.

name: Napier Museum
description: 19th-century museum with Indo-Saracenic architecture housing art, sculptures, and artifacts.

name: Tea Gardens
description: Endless rolling hills carpeted with emerald green tea plantations, Munnar's signature landscape.

name: Eravikulam National Park
description: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr with rolling grasslands and Western Ghats beauty.

name: The Ridge
description: Large open space with panoramic Himalayan views and the iconic Christ Church.

name: Jakhu Temple
description: Ancient temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman atop Jakhu Hill with a 108-foot tall Hanuman statue.

name: Solang Valley
description: Stunning valley for adventure sports — skiing, paragliding, zorbing against snow-capped peaks.

name: Hadimba Temple

name: McLeod Ganj
description: Home of the Dalai Lama and Tibetan government-in-exile, blending Tibetan culture with Himalayan beauty.

name: Robber's Cave
description: Natural cave formation with a river flowing through narrow gorges — walk through cold water inside.

name: Sahastradhara

name: Har Ki Pauri
description: Most sacred Ganges ghat, famous for its spectacular evening Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Laxman Jhula
description: Famous suspension bridge over the Ganges, a spiritual and adventure hub of Uttarakhand.

name: Beatles Ashram
description: Former ashram of Maharishi Mahesh Yogi where The Beatles stayed in 1968, now an artistic landmark.

name: Sanchi Stupa

name: Upper Lake
description: One of India's oldest man-made lakes giving Bhopal its 'City of Lakes' nickname.

name: Rajwada Palace
description: 200-year-old Holkar palace blending Maratha, Mughal, and French architectural styles.

name: Charminar
description: Iconic 16th-century monument with four grand arches and minarets, heart of old city bazaar culture.

name: Golconda Fort
description: Medieval fortress famous for acoustic architecture, diamond mines, and sound-and-light show.

name: Warangal Fort
description: 13th-century Kakatiya dynasty fort with impressive stone gateways and the Thousand Pillar Temple.

name: Kailasagiri
description: Hilltop park with panoramic Bay of Bengal views, massive Shiva-Parvati statue, and ropeway ride.

name: Submarine Museum
description: INS Kurusura, India's first submarine museum housed in a real decommissioned submarine.

name: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
description: The richest and most-visited temple in the world, dedicated to Lord Venkateswara on Tirumala Hills.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage 13th-century temple shaped as a Sun God chariot with 24 carved wheels.

name: Lingaraj Temple
description: 11th-century Odia-style architectural masterpiece, the largest and most important temple in Bhubaneswar.

name: Jagannath Temple
description: One of the four sacred Char Dhams, famous for its annual Rath Yatra with massive wooden chariots.

name: Puri Beach
description: Golden Bay of Bengal beach famous for annual sand art festival and vibrant seafood culture.

name: Chitrakoot Falls
description: 'Niagara of India' — horseshoe-shaped waterfall on Indravati River, the widest in India.

name: Kamakhya Temple
description: Ancient Shakti Peeth atop Nilachal Hill, one of India's most powerful tantric temples.

name: Umananda Island
description: World's smallest river island on the Brahmaputra with a Shiva temple and golden langur monkeys.

name: Kangla Fort
description: Ancient fortification and traditional seat of Manipur's rulers with temples and Kangla Sha dragons.

name: Loktak Lake
description: The largest freshwater lake in northeast India with unique floating phumdis (vegetation islands).

name: Living Root Bridges
description: Natural bridges grown from rubber fig tree roots by Khasi people over centuries, UNESCO nominee.

name: Elephant Falls
description: Beautiful three-tiered waterfall in East Khasi Hills named after an elephant-shaped rock.

name: Tsomgo Lake
description: Glacial lake at 12,310 ft surrounded by snow-capped mountains, color changes with the seasons.

name: Nathula Pass
description: Historic mountain pass at 14,140 ft on the Indo-China border with breathtaking views.

name: Ujjayanta Palace
description: Stunning Mughal-style 1901 palace, now Tripura State Museum surrounded by a Mughal Garden.

name: Ita Fort
description: Ancient 14th-century brick fort whose name gives Itanagar ('City of Bricks') its name.

name: Kohima War Cemetery
description: Beautifully maintained WWII memorial honoring soldiers of the pivotal Battle of Kohima.

name: Durtlang Hills
description: Stunning viewpoint on Aizawl's outskirts with panoramic views of the entire city and valleys.

name: Karna Lake
description: Historical lake named after the Mahabharata warrior Karna, a popular picnic and recreation spot.

name: Kingdom of Dreams
description: India's first live entertainment destination with Bollywood musicals and cultural shows.

name: Cyber Hub
description: Premium dining and entertainment district in DLF CyberCity with over 50 culinary experiences.
